OEM Rack PDU Solutions: Smart Power Distribution Unit for Global Data Centers - Geteknet-Network Cabling Solution Manufacturer

# OEM Rack PDU Solutions: Smart Power Distribution Unit for Global Data Centers

## What Is a PDU?

A Power Distribution Unit (PDU) is a critical device for distributing reliable electricity to multiple IT or telecom equipment within a rack. From basic rack PDU to intelligent PDU, As an experienced OEM manufacturer and supplier, GETEKnet provide a wide range of options to meet data center and enterprise power needs.

## Major Types of PDUs

Basic PDUs deliver straightforward power distribution.

Metered PDUs show real-time load on a display.

Switched or Managed PDUs allow remote outlet control.

Intelligent PDUs (Smart PDUs) integrate monitoring, metering, and network communication for advanced power management.

## Intelligent PDU Features

Smart PDUs support outlet-level metering, environmental sensors (temperature, humidity, airflow), and remote management through SNMP or REST API. These functions enable energy efficiency, capacity planning, and secure power distribution across global facilities.

## Plug Types and International Standards

Rack PDUs are available with a variety of plug and socket combinations to match local requirements. Besides the common IEC C13/C14/C19/C20 outlets, OEM PDUs can be built with:

NEMA connectors for the U.S. and Canada (e.g. NEMA 5-15R, 5-20R, L6-30R).

Schuko (Type F) “German” sockets widely used across Europe.

results.title: Panduit wins prestigious Data Centre Solutions Technology Award
results.text:
Panduit wins prestigious Data Centre Solutions Technology Award | Digitalisation World

# Panduit wins prestigious Data Centre Solutions Technology Award

## For the second consecutive year, Panduit was named a winner at the prestigious DCS Awards, held in London on 22 May 2025.

This year, the award went to the Panduit G6 Power Distribution Unit, a leading PDU developed for data centre environments. Panduit is a global leader in innovative electrical and network infrastructure solutions, and the G6 PDU Series underlines the best-in-class, low profile form factor and enhanced onboard intelligence required in the latest iterations of data centre compute cabinets.

Michael Akinla, Business Manager Central Europe North, Panduit EMEA, commenting on the award, stated, “Gaining industry recognition, such as this excellent DCS Award, for the quality and innovation Panduit products offer our networking and data centre customers is gratifying. It is a strong response to the R&D teams along with the direct installer and customer feedback we gain in the continuous development of the product families.”

The Panduit G6 PDU, is intended to be discrete in the cabinet, and its low profile design saves rack space providing increased airflow to assist live equipment cooling. Today’s data centre environments are driving towards faster performance and increased power utilization, together with environmental, security functions and cybersecurity capabilities, and the Panduit G6 PDU offers best in

queries.query: ODM white label hardware cost premium versus branded manufacturer data center components

results.title: Switching Strategies: Should You Go Branded Switch Or Embrace White Box Switch? - Asterfusion Data Technologies
results.text:
Skip to main content

# Switching Strategies: Should You Go Branded Switch or Embrace White Box Switch?

written by Asterfuison

October 31, 2024

When building a modern data centre or enterprise network, you must make the right choice when it comes to switch hardware. Branded switches are the obvious choice for stability, rich technical support and a mature ecosystem. White-box switches are the new market leaders with their flexible configurations, lower costs and higher customisability. This article will help you make the right choice between brand-name and white-box switches. We will start with the key factors such as cost, flexibility, functional requirements and technical support to help you find the solution that best suits your business needs.

## Branded Switches: A Professional Overview

Branded switches are typically manufactured by renowned network equipment providers such as Cisco, Huawei, Juniper, and HPE. These switches offer a tightly integrated solution, combining hardware and proprietary network operating systems developed by the same company. This integration ensures high stability and reliability, making them a preferred choice for enterprises that prioritize a seamless network experience. Although they often come with a higher price tag, the investment is justified by the renowned brand reputation, stable performance, and robust technical support they offer. Let’s delve into the pros and cons of opting for branded switches:

## Advantages of Branded Switch

results.title: How to Make the Best Choice on White Box Servers for Your Data Center
results.text:
How to Make the Best Choice on White Box Servers for Your Data Center

< Back to PivIT Global Blog

The worldwide server market is finally seeing some growth in sales after a slump for several years. Some may attribute this growth to the increasing popularity of white box servers. Major tech companies like AWS and Facebook have been building their data centers with white box servers.

Such off-brand, customizable servers cater to the specific needs of enterprises, especially those with reliable technical resources for maintenance and repairs.

However, support remains a critical deciding factor for enterprises, turning their preference toward original equipment manufacturers (OEMs).

In this article, we will discuss the following:

- What a white box server is.
- Whether companies should opt for white box servers or stick with OEM.
- An overview of the differences to help you make the best choice for your data center.

Not the article you were looking for today? Try these out:

- Which to Choose? Short-Term vs. Long-Term Maintenance of IT Assets
- 3 Things to Consider Before Opting for OEM Storage Maintenance
- Are Used Servers Worth It? Breaking Down the Pros and Cons

## What Is a White Box Server?

A white box server is an unbranded or generic server with components from different suppliers. These are supplied by original design manufacturers (ODM), who don’t necessarily produce every component of the server but rather put it together using parts from various manufacturers

results.title: The Rise of White-Label Servers: Can SMEs Reduce Their TCO Through the OCP Standard?-ZKKR Technology co.,Ltd
results.text:
The Rise of White-Label Servers: Can SMEs Reduce Their TCO Through the OCP Standard?-ZKKR Technology co.,Ltd



Blog 



The Rise of White-Label Servers: Can SMEs Reduce Their TCO Through the OCP Standard?

---

Release time:

2021-11-12

In recent years, the global server market has been undergoing a structural transformation. Cloud computing giants like Google and Amazon, leading the charge, have significantly boosted their global market share—from 12% in 2015 to 38% in 2024—by directly sourcing white-label servers through the ODM model. This end-user-driven reshaping of the supply chain has not only disrupted the traditional dominance of established brands such as Dell and HP but has also given rise to manufacturing powerhouses like Foxconn Industrial Internet and Inventec, both now valued at over 100 billion yuan. Data shows that in 2024, Taiwan, China, accounted for 81% of global white-label server shipments, with Foxconn playing a pivotal role by supplying more than 50% of NVIDIA’s GB200 super servers.

I. The Market Context Behind the Rise of White-Label Servers

Cloud computing giants like Google and Amazon, leading the charge, have significantly boosted their global market share—from 12% in 2015 to 38% in 2024—by directly procuring white-label servers through the ODM model. This end-user-driven reshaping of the supply chain has not only disrupted the traditional dominance of

# What Are The Benefits Of OEM And ODM Server Components In Networks?

Published by John White on 6 11 月, 2025

OEM (Original Equipment Manufacturer) and ODM (Original Design Manufacturer) server components streamline network scalability, cost efficiency, and customization. OEM parts ensure compatibility with branded systems (e.g., Cisco switches), while ODM solutions enable bespoke designs for specific workloads. Wecent Technology integrates both approaches, using certified components to balance reliability with tailored performance in enterprise networks, reducing downtime and total ownership costs by 15–30%.

What Are The Key Components Of A Server – A Hardware Guide

## How do OEM components enhance network reliability?

OEM server parts like certified CPUs and RAID controllers ensure seamless compatibility with existing infrastructure. Brands like Dell or HP rigorously test components to minimize failure rates below 0.5%—critical for high-availability networks.

⚠️ Warning: Non-OEM drives in branded servers often void warranties and trigger firmware incompatibilities.

Beyond plug-and-play benefits, OEM components simplify firmware updates. For example, HPE Gen10+ servers require OEM SSDs to enable SmartCache acceleration. P

results.title: Why GPU Prices Differ 30%+ for the Same Hardware (and What It Says About the Market) | Mercatus Blog
results.text:
Why GPU Prices Differ 30%+ for the Same Hardware (and What It Says About the Market) | Mercatus Blog

Home Blog Why GPU Prices Differ 30%+ for the Same Hardware (and What It Says About the Market)

GPU EconomicsApr 22, 20269 min read

# Why GPU Prices Differ 30%+ for the Same Hardware (and What It Says About the Market)

GPU prices can vary dramatically across providers for the same hardware. This guide explains why the spread exists, what drives the premium, and what it says about the AI compute market.

M

Mercatus Compute

Author

Share

Two GPU cloud providers serve the exact same NVIDIA H100 SXM5. Same silicon. Same FP8 throughput. Same 80GB HBM3. Same NVLink fabric. One charges $1.99/hour. The other charges $5.00/hour.

The 2.5× spread isn’t a market inefficiency that will resolve next quarter. It’s a structural feature of how AI compute is sold today — and the gap is wider in 2026 than it was two years ago.

This article explains why the spread exists, where the cost differences actually come from, and what it tells you about the underlying market structure. The short answer: the premium isn’t infrastructure cost; it’s sales overhead, margin expectations, and the absence of any public market that would let buyers and sellers find each other efficiently.

## TL;DR

- The hardware itself costs roughly the same at every provider. NVIDIA wholesales H100 SXM5 for similar OEM pricing across customers.
- Hyperscalers’ premium is mostly sales overhead and margin, not infrastru
